name: Savory Beef Stew with Roasted Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1 can Ready-to-serve beef broth (13 to 14.5 oz)
12 medium Mushrooms, cut lengthwise into quarters and seeded
6 Plum tomatoes, each cut lengthwise into quarters and seeded
3 small Onions, each cut lengthwise into quarters
2 tbsp Cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp Water
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: 2.5 hours
1. Trim fat from beef. Cut beef into 1-inch pieces. In a Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat until hot. Add beef and garlic (half at a time) and brown evenly, stirring occasionally. Pour off drippings. Season with 3/4 teaspoon pepper. Stir in broth and dried thyme. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to low. Cover tightly and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours or until beef is tender.
2. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 425°F. Lightly spray a 15 x 10 inch jelly roll pan with vegetable cooking spray. Place vegetables in the pan. Combine 1.5 tablespoons olive oil and 1.5 tablespoons balsamic vinegar; drizzle over vegetables, tossing to coat. Roast in the 425°F oven for 20 to 25 minutes or until tender.
3. Bring beef stew to a boil over medium-high heat. Add cornstarch mixture; cook and stir for 2 minutes or until sauce is slightly thickened and bubbly. Stir in roasted vegetables and remaining balsamic vinegar. Serve with couscous.
Nutrition information per serving:
374 calories
33 g protein
13 g fat (3g saturated fatty acids)
120 calories from total fat (by calories from fat in beef)
82 mg cholesterol
4.9 mg iron
268 mg sodium
